perturbation.original_text: D. Payment. Both Parties agree that once the Parties have agreed to transaction which includes the purchase and sale of the Product that the Buyer will remit payment upon execution of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: D. Payment. Both Parties agree that once the Parties have agreed to transaction which includes the purchase and sale of the Product that the Buyer will remit payment within 15 days of execution of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the payment terms. Originally, payment was due upon execution of the agreement. Now, payment is due within 15 days of execution. This contradicts the immediately following clause about Quarantine.
perturbation.contradicted_text: E. Quarantine. Once the payment has been received, Shi Farms will use its best efforts to quarantine product to ensure safe keeping of the Product until delivery date as agreed by the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated payment was due upon execution, while the changed text states payment is due within 15 days of execution. The contradicted text, Clause E, states that 'Once the payment has been received' Shi Farms will quarantine the product. Since the changed text allows for payment up to 15 days after execution, this directly contradicts Clause E, which assumed immediate payment. This inconsistency creates a strong contradiction regarding when the quarantine period begins, making enforcement unclear and fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
